document.title: Node Browser Canvas Interaction Core (Superseded by v2)
document.purpose: Specify a small reusable Python module that attaches a node-browser style interaction model to a tkinter.Canvas. The module is focused only on canvas interaction, rendering, and a simple projected data model. It is intended to be repointable to different canvas widgets and different data dictionaries.

overview.summary: Implement a procedural, globally-managed canvas interaction system for circular nodes on a tkinter.Canvas. Nodes can be created, dragged, singly selected, marquee-selected in groups, deleted, and connected by curved edges. The system owns only canvas interaction and rendering. External systems may supply the canvas, the data model, and any detail-view behavior.
non-goals: No persistence format in this spec.
No external detail panel behavior in this spec.
No labels or rich metadata beyond node id/name and position.
No zooming, panning, grouping, undo/redo, or multi-canvas synchronization in v1.

architectural-shape: One Python module contains the interaction logic, rendering logic, global state, and active canvas/data references.
The module is externally repointable to a different canvas and/or different data dictionary at runtime.
The module exposes a small procedural surface: attach/use canvas, attach/use data, redraw, and event handlers.
philosophy: Keep the internal data model simple and trusted.
Keep interaction logic direct and easy to inspect.
Structure should be visible from a distance.

module-role.name: Suggested working name: node_canvas_core.py
responsibility: Maintain active references to the current canvas and current graph data.
Handle keyboard and mouse interaction on the active canvas.
Render nodes, selection indicators, marquee rectangle, and connection curves.
Project simple graph data onto the canvas.
external-responsibility: Responding to selection changes if a host program wishes to do so.

data-model.summary: The data model is deliberately small and canvas-oriented.
notes: Node id is both identity and display name for now.
x and y are the logical center of the node.
No other node data is required by this module.
Connection rendering is determined entirely from node positions.

global-state.summary: The module keeps a small set of globals representing active canvas, active data, render settings, and interaction state.

g.drag_mode: NONE or NODE_DRAG or MARQUEE_DRAG or GROUP_DRAG

rules: Globals are mutated, not rebound wholesale, except when intentionally repointing active canvas or graph data.
Structured state should live in dedicated named globals rather than being stuffed into one catch-all bag.
The module should remain inspectable during runtime.

canvas-binding-model.summary: The module is attachable to a canvas and repointable to another.
behavior: use_canvas(canvas) sets the active canvas reference and binds all required events.
If another canvas was active previously, that old canvas should be unbound first.
use_graph_data(graph_data) sets the active graph reference and redraws.
The module assumes only one active canvas at a time.

modes.name: CREATE_NODE
modes.meaning: After pressing N, the next click on empty space creates a node and returns the system to IDLE.

notes: The mode should be explicit and easy to inspect.
CREATE_NODE is one-shot. Pressing N arms one placement only.

modifier-based-modes.name: EDGE_CREATE_DRAG
modifier-based-modes.activation: Shift + mouse down on a node
modifier-based-modes.note: This is not a persistent mode; it exists only during the drag gesture.

single-selection.definition: At most one node may be the selected node for detail-view purposes.
behavior: Clicking a node without dragging makes it the selected node.
Clicking empty space may clear the selected node.
Dragging a node does not by itself make it the selected node.

group-selection.definition: A separate set of zero or more nodes selected for group operations such as dragging and deleting.
behavior: Marquee selection fills the group-selected set.
Group selection does not imply or replace single selected node.
There may be a selected node that is not part of the group-selected set.
Delete acts on group selection if group selection is non-empty; otherwise on selected node if any.

node-creation.keyboard: Pressing N while the active canvas has focus enters CREATE_NODE mode.
mouse: In CREATE_NODE mode, the next click creates a node at that position.
After creation, the node becomes the selected node.

node-id-generation.default: If host does not supply a node id generator, module may generate simple ids like node-0001, node-0002, ...
node-id-generation.preferred: Allow optional external callback or procedure for creating the next node id.

notes: Creation does not require a pre-existing selected node.
The newly created node need not automatically become the selected node unless explicitly chosen later by the host or desired in a later version.
configurability: Auto-select behavior should be implemented in a single clearly named step (e.g. select_single_node(new_id)) so it can be easily removed or toggled later.

node-hit.rule: A point hits a node if its distance from the node center is less than or equal to node_radius plus hit_slop.

priority: Node hit-testing should be based on graph data geometry, not canvas item ids alone.
If multiple nodes overlap, the last-drawn/topmost node should win unless a different policy is later introduced.

plain-click-on-node: Mouse down on node records candidate click.
If mouse up occurs without meaningful drag, set selected_node_id to that node.
drag-node: Mouse down on a node and moving beyond drag threshold begins NODE_DRAG unless group-drag conditions apply.
During NODE_DRAG, the node center follows pointer movement by delta.
On release, drag ends and redraw occurs.
group-drag: If mouse down occurs on a node that is inside the current group selection, then dragging may move the whole group together.
All group-selected nodes move by the same delta.
marquee-drag: Mouse down on empty canvas in IDLE begins marquee candidate.
Dragging on empty canvas draws a rectangular marquee.
On mouse release, all nodes whose centers fall within the marquee rectangle become the new group-selected set.

drag-threshold.purpose: Prevent a tiny accidental movement from being interpreted as a drag.

gesture: Mouse down on a source node.
Release over a target node to create an edge.
behavior: On SHIFT + mouse down on node A: begin edge-drag with source A.
During drag: optionally render a preview line from A to current mouse position.
On release over node B (B != A): create edge {from: A, to: B}.
On release not over a node: cancel edge creation.
On release over same node: no-op (or optionally ignore).
rendering: During edge drag, draw a temporary preview curve or line from source node to pointer.
Preview is not part of graph_data and should be cleared on release.
priority: SHIFT + click on node takes precedence over normal drag or selection.
While in EDGE_CREATE_DRAG, no node movement or selection change occurs.

requirements: The active canvas must be able to receive keyboard focus.
Clicking the canvas when it does not have focus should focus it and consume the click (no selection or other action).
Subsequent clicks when the canvas already has focus may perform normal interaction.
N and Delete act only when the canvas has focus.

idle-click: If canvas does NOT have focus: clicking focuses the canvas and does nothing else.
If canvas already has focus: a completed click (mouse down and up without drag) clears single selection.
Click-drag on empty canvas creates marquee selection.

click-and-drag-behavior.click-definition: A click is defined as mouse down followed by mouse up without exceeding drag threshold.
click-and-drag-behavior.note: Selection clearing only occurs on a completed click, not on drag initiation.

idle-click: Clicking empty canvas in IDLE may clear single selection.
Click-drag on empty canvas creates marquee selection instead of panning.
create-node-click: Clicking empty canvas in CREATE_NODE places a node.

delete-behavior.keyboard: Delete key removes selected items according to priority.
priority: If group_selected_ids is non-empty, delete all nodes in that set.
Else if selected_node_id is not null, delete that single node.
edge-cleanup: Deleting a node also deletes any edges whose from or to refers to that node.
selection-cleanup: Deleted nodes are removed from selected_node_id and group_selected_ids as needed.

edge-rendering.summary: Connections are directional and visually flow downward from the source node toward the target node.

geometry.start-point: Bottom of source node: (from.x, from.y + node_radius)
geometry.end-point: Top of target node: (to.x, to.y - node_radius)

curve-style.preferred: Smooth cubic-like curve using Canvas smooth line support with manually chosen control points approximated by intermediate points.

notes: The exact curve need not be mathematically pure Bézier if Canvas smooth mode yields the intended visual result.

rendering-model.summary: Canvas contents are treated as a projection of graph data plus transient interaction overlays.

normal: Outer circle only, white thick outline, no fill or background fill depending on style setting.
selected: Normal node rendering plus dark green interior fill or selected interior effect.
Smaller inner circle at radius 20 with thin outline to indicate selected-node status.
group-selected: Nodes in the group-selected set should display a thin blue halo.
The halo is an additional outer ring slightly larger than the node radius (e.g. radius + 3 pixels).
Halo line width should be thin (1–2 pixels).
Halo color should be a clear blue distinct from selected-node green.
This styling is independent of selected-node styling.

background: Canvas background color should be configurable.
Default may be black for strong contrast with white nodes and edges.

coordinate-policy.definition: Node x,y are always center coordinates in canvas space.
movement: The graph data is the source of truth; canvas geometry is regenerated from it.

required-procedures-detail.use_canvas(canvas): Set active canvas, bind events, ensure focus behavior is possible, redraw if graph is already present.
required-procedures-detail.use_graph_data(graph_data): Set active graph reference and redraw.
required-procedures-detail.redraw_all(): Rebuild visible canvas representation from graph_data and current interaction state.
required-procedures-detail.create_node_at(x, y): Create node entry in graph_data and redraw.
required-procedures-detail.delete_selected(): Delete group or single selection according to policy and redraw.
required-procedures-detail.find_node_at(x, y): Return node id or null using geometry-based hit testing.

behavior.Escape: May cancel CREATE_NODE mode and clear marquee-in-progress.

interaction-state-machine.summary: A small explicit state machine is preferred over implicit branching chaos.

states.name: IDLE
transitions: mouse-down on empty space -> MARQUEE candidate

states.name: CREATE_NODE
transitions: click on canvas -> create node, then IDLE

states.name: CLICK_OR_DRAG_NODE
transitions: small movement + release -> select node, then IDLE
drag threshold crossed -> NODE_DRAG or GROUP_DRAG

states.name: MARQUEE_DRAG
transitions: release -> commit group selection, then IDLE

states.name: EDGE_CREATE_DRAG
transitions: release over node -> create edge, then IDLE

interaction-state-machine.note: Implementation may encode these as strings in globals rather than formal objects.

requirements: The active canvas must be able to receive keyboard focus.
Clicking the canvas should typically focus it.
N and Delete should act only when the canvas is the intended target.

optional-callbacks.generate_node_id(): Host may supply new node ids.
optional-callbacks.on_graph_mutated(): Host may be notified after structural or positional changes.

notes: Callbacks are optional. The module should function without them.
Host integration should not be entangled with core interaction logic.

future-extensions: Connection-creation interaction mode, e.g. press C then click source and target.
Distinct visual styling for group-selected nodes.
Node labels drawn next to or inside nodes.
acceptance-criteria: A canvas can be attached to the module and made active.
A graph data dictionary can be attached to the module and rendered.
Pressing N then clicking empty canvas creates exactly one node and exits create mode.
Clicking a node selects it without dragging it.
Dragging a node moves it without necessarily changing selected node.
Dragging empty space creates a marquee and produces a group selection on release.
Dragging a node inside the current group selection moves the full group.
Delete removes group selection first, otherwise selected node.
Deleting a node also removes attached edges.
Edges render as downward-flowing curves from source bottom to target top.
The module can be repointed to a different canvas and different graph data.

style: Use procedural functions, named globals, and explicit state.
Prefer geometry-based hit detection from graph_data rather than deriving truth from canvas item ids.
Redraw from graph state rather than incrementally micro-patching every visual detail unless that becomes necessary later.
coding-shape: Small named functions are preferred over one giant event handler.
The code should read like a story of the interaction.

interaction-priority: Canvas focus acquisition consumes the first click.
SHIFT-modified gestures take precedence over normal node drag or selection.
Drag threshold distinguishes click vs drag.
Group drag takes precedence when dragging a node inside group selection.
Marquee applies only when starting from empty space.
